retrieved.title: University in a nutshell
retrieved.text:
On this site prospective and first-year students get a first insight into the University of Vienna and into student life.

A university degree mainly differs from school and other vocational trainings in two aspects:

1. I am myself responsible for my study progress. This means, for example, that I have to organise my time and schedule my semester on my own.
2. A university degree does not prepare me for a specific profession, as for example an apprenticeship does. It is a basic academic training in the selected field. I get to know scientific theories and working methods which may open up several career opportunities. I gain professional practice and experience for my later profession while working besides studying, or during an internship.

Our videos in the category "Studying made easier" explain vividly important terms from student life at the University of Vienna.

In the "Uni Stories" students talk about their everyday life at university. The stories contain different topics, such as housing, budget, finances, stays abroad, barrier-free studying or the reasons that were decisive for the students choice of a particular study program.

- [University Know-How](#c274210)
- [Uni Stories](#c345104)

heading_path: Your steps in the admission procedure
retrieved.text:
1. Create your u:account in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) withing the application period. Make sure to remember your login data. You will need your u:account during your studies.
2. Log into u:space to complete the registration.
3. At "non-degree programme" you will find a link for the [Statistical Data Confirmation (UHStat1)](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/statistische-daten). Fill in the questionnaire and choose "Check and submit".

#### Registration in u:space

1. Log into the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/2148) with your u:account and let us know that you want to be admitted to a non-degree programme.
2. Once you have been (preliminarily) admitted, you will receive an e-mail containing important information. Please read this e-mail carefully. Please note: The University of Vienna reserves the right to invite you to an [online appointment](/en/admission/online-first-time-admission/).
3. Pay the [tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/). The admission is completed as soon as the prescribed fee is received in u:space > Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. Afterwards, you can download your study documents (student record sheet and confirmation of enrolment) in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Personal matters > My documents.

As soon as your tuition fee has been received by the University of Vienna, you can order your student ID card, the [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en).

Register for the courses you want to attend. The responsible [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/ StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) will gladly help you with any questions regarding registration for courses and examination.

- Can I attend courses, even if I do not have a secondary-school leaving certificate?

  Yes. You can be admitted to a non-degree programme to attend individual courses if you do not have a secondary-school leaving certificate. You do not have to fulfil any special admission requirements.
- How much does a non-degree programme cost?

  In a non-degree programme you have to pay the [tuition fee as well as the Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/), irrespective of your citizenship.
- Which courses can I attend?

  Please inquire at the [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) or StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of the degree programme that offers the courses you are interested in. retrieved.title: Co-registration at the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
Are you studying at another Austrian university, but would like to complete individual courses at the University of Vienna? You can have these courses recognised at your home university by applying for co-registration. Don't forget to register for courses after your application has been approved.

Please note:

- You cannot complete a degree programme at the University of Vienna as a co-registered student.
- The co-registration has to be applied for each semester.
- If you are enrolled in a Doctoral/PhD programme and want to co-register, please contact the [Center for Doctoral Studies](https://doktorat.univie.ac.at/en/doctoralphd-programmes/co-registration/).

If you are studying at the University of Vienna and would like to take courses at another university, please contact the [StudyServiceCentre (SSC) or the StudyServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of your degree programme.

You need the following documents as PDF files:

- [the pre-approval form (PDF)](/fileadmin/user_upload/p_studieren/Studienzulassung/Formulare/Pre-approval_of_the_co-registration.pdf)
- the student record sheet of the current semester (the semester, in which you want to co-register)
- your student ID card, valid passport or personal ID

retrieved.title: Our offers and projects
heading_path: School meets Uni - Offers for school classes
retrieved.text:
The Vienna University Library is open to all high school students free of charge. It supports students with their research for the ABA (formerly VWA) or the BHS diploma thesis. School classes can attend library courses free of charge. The students get to know the university library. They learn how to get a library card and how to borrow books. They are also introduced to research strategies using the central media search engine u:search.

- [Library courses for school classes](https://bibliothek.univie.ac.at/schuelerinnen-schulungen.html)

If you are interested, you can also book a guided tour through the Main Building of the University of Vienna. The guided tour through the Main Building cost €35,-- per school class. A maximum of 30 people per group can participate in the tour. To arrange a tour, please send an email to fuehrungen@univie.ac.at. A combination of guided tour and lecture is the best way to get to know the University of Vienna.

© Universität Wien

retrieved.title: Studying a non-degree programme (again)
retrieved.text:
You were admitted to the University of Vienna in the past and want to be admitted to a non-degree programme now? Non-degree students are not admitted to a specific degree programme but, for example, to the "attending of individual courses" or to a continuing education programme.

You were admitted at the University of Vienna in the past and want to visit individual courses out of interest? If this is the case, you have to note several things:

- Examination attempts in the non-degree programme are also counted in the degree programme. If all examination attempts are graded negatively, you will be permanently excluded from the degree programme, and you cannot study this degree programme.
- You have to pay the [tuition fee as well as the Students’ Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/).
- Course places are preferably distributed among degree students.
- During a non-degree programme it is not possible to pass (partial) diploma examinations, module examinations or public defenses.

Message us via the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) within the application period and attach a scan of your passport or personal ID card. Please also send us the certificate of your name change, if applicable. We will admit you to the non-degree programme and inform you about further steps via e-mail. The processing may take several days.

Another option is the admission to a bachelor/diploma or master programme, if you fulfil the admission requirements.

retrieved.title: Information for asylum seekers
retrieved.text:
Through the process of nostrification, a foreign university degree can get recognised as equivalent to an Austrian university degree programme. This recognition also entails all rights and duties that follow, such as bearing an academic title and having permission to purse a certain profession, among others.

The official Austrian information centre for international nostrifications in the university field is [ENIC NARIC AUSTRIA](https://www.aais.at/).

You may file for nostrification at the [office of the Studienpräses](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/en/nostrification/).

In order to apply for admission to another degree programme, you do not have to have your higher education degree recognised through nostrification.

Once you have been admitted to a degree programme, you may [plan your semester](/en/semester-planning/) and schedule your classes.

If you are in need of assistance, you may reach out to the [StudiesServiceCenters (SSC) or StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of your degree programme. The [Student representatives (STV)](https://oeh.univie.ac.at/vertretung/fakultaets-und-studienvertretungen) of your degree programme can also help you with questions related to organising your studies.

During your studies, the [Center for Teaching and Learning (CTL)](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/en/) will offer valuable support. The [STEOP-Mentoring](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/en/services-for-students/your-first-semester/steop-mentoring/) programme will help you particularly at the beginning of your studies. Any questions or concerns that you may have can be addressed there, while mentors will also provide insights about organising your study programme and exams as well as useful advice on reading and studying techniques.

retrieved.title: Why study at the University of Vienna?
retrieved.text:
Studying at the University of Vienna means being part of a large and diverse community. Students from around 140 different countries are currently pursuing a degree at the University of Vienna. Our participation in networks such as Erasmus+ and partnerships with the best universities in the world open up numerous exciting exchange opportunities. Diversity is encouraged by applying the principle of equal opportunities in all areas of university activity.

reranked.title: University in a nutshell
reranked.text:
On this site prospective and first-year students get a first insight into the University of Vienna and into student life.

A university degree mainly differs from school and other vocational trainings in two aspects:

1. I am myself responsible for my study progress. This means, for example, that I have to organise my time and schedule my semester on my own.
2. A university degree does not prepare me for a specific profession, as for example an apprenticeship does. It is a basic academic training in the selected field. I get to know scientific theories and working methods which may open up several career opportunities. I gain professional practice and experience for my later profession while working besides studying, or during an internship.

Our videos in the category "Studying made easier" explain vividly important terms from student life at the University of Vienna.

In the "Uni Stories" students talk about their everyday life at university. The stories contain different topics, such as housing, budget, finances, stays abroad, barrier-free studying or the reasons that were decisive for the students choice of a particular study program.

- [University Know-How](#c274210)
- [Uni Stories](#c345104)

1. Create your u:account in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) withing the application period. Make sure to remember your login data. You will need your u:account during your studies.
2. Log into u:space to complete the registration.
3. At "non-degree programme" you will find a link for the [Statistical Data Confirmation (UHStat1)](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/statistische-daten). Fill in the questionnaire and choose "Check and submit".

3. Pay the [tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/). The admission is completed as soon as the prescribed fee is received in u:space > Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. Afterwards, you can download your study documents (student record sheet and confirmation of enrolment) in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Personal matters > My documents.

As soon as your tuition fee has been received by the University of Vienna, you can order your student ID card, the [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en).

reranked.text:
Are you studying at another Austrian university, but would like to complete individual courses at the University of Vienna? You can have these courses recognised at your home university by applying for co-registration. Don't forget to register for courses after your application has been approved.

Please note:

- You cannot complete a degree programme at the University of Vienna as a co-registered student.
- The co-registration has to be applied for each semester.
- If you are enrolled in a Doctoral/PhD programme and want to co-register, please contact the [Center for Doctoral Studies](https://doktorat.univie.ac.at/en/doctoralphd-programmes/co-registration/).

If you are studying at the University of Vienna and would like to take courses at another university, please contact the [StudyServiceCentre (SSC) or the StudyServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of your degree programme.

You need the following documents as PDF files:

- [the pre-approval form (PDF)](/fileadmin/user_upload/p_studieren/Studienzulassung/Formulare/Pre-approval_of_the_co-registration.pdf)

The Vienna University Library is open to all high school students free of charge. It supports students with their research for the ABA (formerly VWA) or the BHS diploma thesis. School classes can attend library courses free of charge. The students get to know the university library. They learn how to get a library card and how to borrow books. A combination of guided tour and lecture is the best way to get to know the University of Vienna.

reranked.title: Studying a non-degree programme (again)
reranked.text:
You were admitted to the University of Vienna in the past and want to be admitted to a non-degree programme now? Non-degree students are not admitted to a specific degree programme but, for example, to the "attending of individual courses" or to a continuing education programme.

You were admitted at the University of Vienna in the past and want to visit individual courses out of interest? If this is the case, you have to note several things:

- Examination attempts in the non-degree programme are also counted in the degree programme. If all examination attempts are graded negatively, you will be permanently excluded from the degree programme, and you cannot study this degree programme.
- You have to pay the [tuition fee as well as the Students’ Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/).
- Course places are preferably distributed among degree students.
- During a non-degree programme it is not possible to pass (partial) diploma examinations, module examinations or public defenses.

Message us via the [contact form](/en/contact-form/) within the application period and attach a scan of your passport or personal ID card. Please also send us the certificate of your name change, if applicable. We will admit you to the non-degree programme and inform you about further steps via e-mail. The processing may take several days.

Another option is the admission to a bachelor/diploma or master programme, if you fulfil the admission requirements.

reranked.title: Information for asylum seekers
reranked.text:
Through the process of nostrification, a foreign university degree can get recognised as equivalent to an Austrian university degree programme. This recognition also entails all rights and duties that follow, such as bearing an academic title and having permission to purse a certain profession, among others.

The official Austrian information centre for international nostrifications in the university field is [ENIC NARIC AUSTRIA](https://www.aais.at/).

You may file for nostrification at the [office of the Studienpräses](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/en/nostrification/).

In order to apply for admission to another degree programme, you do not have to have your higher education degree recognised through nostrification.

Once you have been admitted to a degree programme, you may [plan your semester](/en/semester-planning/) and schedule your classes.

If you are in need of assistance, you may reach out to the [StudiesServiceCenters (SSC) or StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of your degree programme. The [Student representatives (STV)](https://oeh.univie.ac.at/vertretung/fakultaets-und-studienvertretungen) of your degree programme can also help you with questions related to organising your studies.

During your studies, the [Center for Teaching and Learning (CTL)](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/en/) will offer valuable support. The [STEOP-Mentoring](https://ctl.univie.ac.at/en/services-for-students/your-first-semester/steop-mentoring/) programme will help you particularly at the beginning of your studies. Any questions or concerns that you may have can be addressed there, while mentors will also provide insights about organising your study programme and exams as well as useful advice on reading and studying techniques.

reranked.title: Why study at the University of Vienna?
reranked.text:
Studying at the University of Vienna means being part of a large and diverse community. Students from around 140 different countries are currently pursuing a degree at the University of Vienna. Our participation in networks such as Erasmus+ and partnerships with the best universities in the world open up numerous exciting exchange opportunities. Diversity is encouraged by applying the principle of equal opportunities in all areas of university activity.

- [#univie communities](/en/student-life-who-can-help-me-with/univie-communities/#c487599)
- [Summer & Winter Schools](https://international.univie.ac.at/en/univie-summer/winter-schools)
- [Student Mobility](https://international.univie.ac.at/en/)
- [Diversity portal of the University of Vienna](https://gleichstellung.univie.ac.at/en/diversity)
- [Accessible studying at the University of Vienna](https://barrierefrei.univie.ac.at/) (in German)

© University of Vienna / Barbara Mair

© University of Vienna / derknopfdruecker.com

The University of Vienna is also the largest research and educational institution in Austria with a total of 7,400 academics. Our teaching staff adheres to the principle of research-led teaching in their courses. They impart knowledge about latest research findings and their students are able to participate in original research. The University of Vienna sets future-oriented accents in interdisciplinary innovative research fields.

- [Financial support by the University of Vienna](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/stipendien/) (in German)
- [Research Services and Career Development](https://forschung.univie.ac.at/en/)
- [The University of Vienna´s strategic priorities](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/research)

selected.title: University in a nutshell
selected.text:
On this site prospective and first-year students get a first insight into the University of Vienna and into student life.

A university degree mainly differs from school and other vocational trainings in two aspects:

1. I am myself responsible for my study progress. This means, for example, that I have to organise my time and schedule my semester on my own.
2. A university degree does not prepare me for a specific profession, as for example an apprenticeship does. It is a basic academic training in the selected field. I get to know scientific theories and working methods which may open up several career opportunities. I gain professional practice and experience for my later profession while working besides studying, or during an internship.

Our videos in the category "Studying made easier" explain vividly important terms from student life at the University of Vienna.

In the "Uni Stories" students talk about their everyday life at university. The stories contain different topics, such as housing, budget, finances, stays abroad, barrier-free studying or the reasons that were decisive for the students choice of a particular study program.

- [University Know-How](#c274210)
- [Uni Stories](#c345104)

© Universität Wien/ Martin Sowa

selected.title: Attending courses out of interest
heading_path: Your steps in the admission procedure
selected.text:
1. Create your u:account in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) withing the application period. Make sure to remember your login data. You will need your u:account during your studies.
2. Log into u:space to complete the registration.
3. At "non-degree programme" you will find a link for the [Statistical Data Confirmation (UHStat1)](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/statistische-daten). Fill in the questionnaire and choose "Check and submit".

#### Registration in u:space

1. Log into the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/2148) with your u:account and let us know that you want to be admitted to a non-degree programme.
2. Once you have been (preliminarily) admitted, you will receive an e-mail containing important information. Please read this e-mail carefully. Please note: The University of Vienna reserves the right to invite you to an [online appointment](/en/admission/online-first-time-admission/).
3. Pay the [tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/). The admission is completed as soon as the prescribed fee is received in u:space > Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. Afterwards, you can download your study documents (student record sheet and confirmation of enrolment) in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) > Personal matters > My documents.

As soon as your tuition fee has been received by the University of Vienna, you can order your student ID card, the [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en).

Register for the courses you want to attend. The responsible [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/ StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) will gladly help you with any questions regarding registration for courses and examination.

- Can I attend courses, even if I do not have a secondary-school leaving certificate?

  Yes. You can be admitted to a non-degree programme to attend individual courses if you do not have a secondary-school leaving certificate. You do not have to fulfil any special admission requirements.
- How much does a non-degree programme cost?

  In a non-degree programme you have to pay the [tuition fee as well as the Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/), irrespective of your citizenship.
- Which courses can I attend?

  Please inquire at the [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) or StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of the degree programme that offers the courses you are interested in. Information on individual courses is available in [u:find](http://ufind.univie.ac.at/en), the course and staff directory of the University of Vienna.

Rules:
- Base every statement on the sources; never add outside knowledge or guess.

   Once you have been (preliminarily) admitted, you will receive an e-mail containing important information. Please read this e-mail carefully. Please note: The University of Vienna reserves the right to invite you to an [online appointment](/en/admission/online-first-time-admission/).
4. Pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee.
   The admission is completed as soon as the [prescribed fee](/en/tuition-fee/) is received in u:space > Financial Matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee. Afterwards, you can download your study documents (student record sheet and confirmation of enrolment) in u:space > Personal matters > My documents.

As soon as your tuition fee has been received by the University of Vienna, you can validate your student ID card, the [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en) or order it if you do not yet have one.

Register for the required courses. The responsible [StudiesServiceCenter (SSC)/ StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)](https://www.univie.ac.at/ueber-uns/leitung-organisation/studien-lehrorganisation/) will gladly help you with any questions regarding registration for courses and examination.

Question: How does a student from another Austrian university take a course at Uni Vienna?
